[Coffee Mate MCP Server](../../../../index.md) / [transport/stdio/stdio](../index.md) / startStdioServer

# startStdioServer()

> **Transport Layer**

```ts
function startStdioServer(server: McpServer): Effect<void>;
```

Defined in: [src/app/transport/stdio/stdio.ts:61](https://github.com/EmperorRAG/first-mcp/blob/3d2831b148eaa4c84c57fa062fce6df920f72a50/src/app/transport/stdio/stdio.ts#L61)

Connects an [McpServer](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/typescript-sdk) to a [StdioServerTransport](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/typescript-sdk) and
begins listening for JSON-RPC messages on `process.stdin`, writing
responses to `process.stdout`.

## Parameters

| Parameter | Type                                                                  | Description                                                                                                                                    |
| --------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `server`  | [`McpServer`](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/typescript-sdk) | A fully-configured [McpServer](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/typescript-sdk) instance with all domains and tools already registered. |

## Returns

[`Effect`](https://effect-ts.github.io/effect/)<`void`>

An [Effect.Effect](https://effect-ts.github.io/effect/) that resolves once the transport is
connected and the server is ready to receive messages.

## Remarks

This transport is activated when the application is launched with the
`--stdio` CLI flag (see `main.ts`).  It is the primary integration
path for local VS Code MCP clients configured via `.vscode/mcp.json`.

Internally the function:

1. Instantiates a [StdioServerTransport](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/typescript-sdk) — the MCP SDK class
   that reads newline-delimited JSON-RPC from `stdin` and writes
   responses to `stdout`.
2. Connects the provided [McpServer](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/typescript-sdk) to the transport via
   [McpServer.connect](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/typescript-sdk), which begins the bidirectional message
   loop.
3. Logs a confirmation message via [Effect.logInfo](https://effect-ts.github.io/effect/).

Because stdio is inherently single-session (one `stdin`/`stdout` pair),
no session map or transport cleanup is required — the process lifecycle
manages the connection.

## Example

```ts
import { Effect } from "effect";
import { startStdioServer } from "./stdio.js";

const server = createMcpServer();
await Effect.runPromise(startStdioServer(server));
```
